title: narcissus neglectus subsp. spiralis
intro: Narcissus neglectus subsp. spiralis, also known as the Wild Jonquil, is a rare and lesser-known species of the narcissus plant family, characterized by its unique, twisted and curved leaves and delicate white flowers. Traditionally, the bulbs of Narcissus neglectus subsp. spiralis have been used in herbal medicine due to their potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with this plant are believed to include the relief of symptoms related to respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, and the promotion of a healthy digestive system. Narcissus neglectus subsp. spiralis is typically prepared for medicinal use in the form of dried bulbs, extracts, or infusions, which can be consumed as teas, tinctures, or added to other herbal remedies.
